PS-DCR-002.author: S. Krause
PS-DCR-002.text: The step should record the actual verification threshold: knowledge-based verification uses a fixed set of three security questions and the caller must answer at least two correctly to pass. As written it just says security questions, with no number and no pass mark — an auditor will ask.

PS-DCR-002.author: Process Specialist
PS-DCR-002.text:
Comment review (Process Specialist).

Incorporated — S. Krause's note on the missing verification threshold. The "What happens" block now records that phone-channel verification asks a fixed set of three security questions and requires at least two correct answers to pass, instead of the generic "security questions". The edit re-opened the element's approval; S. Krause to re-approve on the card. The threshold also belongs in CP-DCR-001's control activity — flagged separately, out of scope for this single-element review.

M-DCR-003.author: S. Krause
M-DCR-003.text: Should we change the delivery metric target to 3 business days? It feels slow.

M-DCR-003.author: Process Specialist
M-DCR-003.text:
Comment review (Process Specialist).

Declined — S. Krause's note proposing a 3-business-day delivery target. M-DCR-003 is a current-state metric: its 5-7 business-day target records what the process commits to today, sourced from the DTP service-level table. A faster target is a transformation ambition, not a correction of the as-is record, and editing the metric here would misstate the current commitment. Reason for declining: the figure is accurate for the As-Is; raise the faster target as a transformation-decision in the Target Process area.
